sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal for the Cardano Builder DAO, described as a smart contract-governed funding mechanism to allocate capital to builders advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs.
Recipient: Cardano Builder DAO, with withdrawal administration by an independent council/multisig composed of dRep DAO members before milestone-based disbursement.

Deliverables: Operate a builder-led funding pathway for Cardano projects, Evaluate proposals, coordinate feedback, distribute funds transparently, and track funded project results, Require funded projects to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions, Continue development of KPI dashboards and move toward live on-chain KPI tracking, Disburse milestones only after administrator validation and confirmation

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says Cardano Builder DAO is designed to fund builders who can improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL for Cardano.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The document states that funded projects are required to track core KPIs including monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer claims the DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied directly to live on-chain Cardano data.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer states the DAO has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The document claims Round 1 funded 20 companies, involved 38 voting members and 27 requesting members, distributed 5,541,335 ADA, and achieved 83% governance participation.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The document claims Round 2 funded 14 companies, added 18 new members, and achieved 88% member participation after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposer claims the DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied directly to live on-chain Cardano data.
Independent evidence for: The proposer states the DAO has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.
Independent evidence for: The document claims Round 1 funded 20 companies, involved 38 voting members and 27 requesting members, distributed 5,541,335 ADA, and achieved 83% governance participation.
Independent evidence for: The document claims Round 2 funded 14 companies, added 18 new members, and achieved 88% member participation after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal says Cardano Builder DAO is designed to fund builders who can improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL for Cardano." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposer claims the DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied directly to live on-chain Cardano data." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
